tweets.full_text:
Claude CodeやCursorと何が違うのかって話なんだけど、ポイントは16エージェントが並列で動くHeavyアーキテクチャと200万トークンのコンテキスト窓。要は1つのプロンプトで大きなコードベースを丸ごと理解しながら複数ファイルをまとめて書き換えられる設計になってる。

インストールはcurlコマンド1本で、SuperGrokアカウントで認証するだけっていうシンプルさ。変更は全部diffで表示されるから、プランを承認してステップごとにコメント入れてっていう、開発者が安心できるフローなのは好印象。

ただ$99/月でもClaude Code ProやCursorの$20/月と比べるとだいぶ高い。200万トークンのコンテキストが活きるのは大規模プロジェクトだけで、個人開発だとオーバースペック感がある。ツールが増えても使いこなす側の設計力が問われるのは結局変わらない。

ソース: https://t.co/yX0UKVn8yw

user.name: MiSteR Kitti
user.description: Detecting internal strain when LLMs commit to tokens — zero training, single ➡️pass.

tweets.full_text:
Hermes hit 50K GitHub stars in 2 months. now it talks to Claude Code 

This operator pulls $14k/month running outbound for 8 clients on the combined stack

Most builders still run them as separate apps

That's the gap..

Here's the math:

> Apollo + Hermes + Claude Code stack: ~$200/month total
> Outbound agency rate per client: $1,500-2,500/month
> Operators running 8 clients on the combined stack: $12,000-20,000/month
> Context switches eliminated per day: 30-50
> Setup with Pantheon persona templates: under 2 hours

5 features the combo unlocks that neither has alone:

1. Pantheon personas - 5-7 specialized agents (sales, copy, research, deploy, ops) share one Hermes memory. Switch character mid-conversation, no context reset

2. Obsidian as shared memory - write once in your daily note, Hermes and Claude Code both read it. One source of truth replaces 6 prompt files

3. visual intelligence - Claude Code drops a screenshot, Hermes parses it, the next agent acts on it. Images stop being a dead-end

4. cron-scheduled outbound - Apollo lead scrape at 6am, Hermes drafts personalized DMs by 7am, Claude Code stages them for approval by 8am. You wake up to 50 ready-to-send messages

5. universal command surface - type "show me yesterday's wins" in either Hermes or Claude Code, you get the same answer. No more "which tool did I save it in."

In 12 months, the gap between operators running Hermes and Claude as separate apps and operators running them as one OS won't be model choice

It'll be how well they wired the operating system underneath

📌 Full link to the video: https://t.co/GZqPajcyP8

The full setup with personas, prompt files, and Obsidian template is in the article below 👇

tweets.full_text:
We serve a lot of other investment firms, and they'd echo this -- MCPs with good data really take agents to the next level. 

E.g. prompts like "go through every software company and rate the AI bear case by credibility" don't work with vanilla Claude Code, but with a good MCP that provides qualitative data, you actually get good analysis on 250+ companies

tweets.full_text:
There are a lot of small differences between the Claude Code and Codex CLIs:

- Different skill names and shortcuts
- Different built-ins (ex. docs, browser testing)
- Subtle gaps in featureset (ex. available hooks)

Here's what you need to know: https://t.co/rDHopOvSaY

user.name: Ben Holmes
user.description: The Whiteboard Guy @warpdotdev

tweets.full_text:
Semantic skill loading — no keywords, no manual wiring.

Local embeddings. Cosine similarity scoring. Only the best match activates. LRU eviction for long sessions.

Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider — all make you wire this up by hand. Every. Single. Session.

tweets.full_text:
Current flow:
Claude code starts -> reads config -> connect to MCPs -> session begins
Right now we have to manually restart session to load MCP…

“hot – reload”:
Session running -> skill triggers -> MCP spins up -> tools available immediately -> MCP shuts down when done

user.description: The home for honest takes on the platform and the creator economy.

tweets.full_text:
Simply because of the token cost, I modified the skill to work with Claude Code as well since they just reset limits.

It’s much much slower of course and Peter probably wouldn’t be happy 😂

But I’m interested to see the results for budget purposes because I can’t afford the token cost of this yet on Codex long term. It truly does work on an insane level, it’s just too expensive for me right now.

My goal (I’ll be tracking the costs + speed difference) is to have Claude catch the majority of obvious booboos and fix it. Document it. Then when I go to Codex for final review, it will know what Claude did already and the cost drops.

Will report back.

🤞🏻
